title: Component Service
description: A root-level utility service for dynamically creating Angular components and embedded views from templates at runtime. It provides the low-level building block used by overlay, popup, and other dynamic UI layers in AcoreX.
imports: import { AXComponentService, AXComponentType, AXComponentContent, AXComponentInputs, AXComponentOptions } from '@acorex/core/components';

hints.title: Low-level API
hints.content: AXComponentService renders content into the application's root ViewContainerRef. For most use cases — modals, popovers, tooltips, and toasts — prefer AXOverlayService or AXPopupService, which handle positioning, backdrop, z-index, and disposal for you.

hints.title: Cleanup
hints.content: Always destroy dynamically created components and embedded views when they are no longer needed. Call componentRef.destroy() or embeddedViewRef.destroy() to avoid memory leaks.

types.title: AXComponentService
types.description: Injectable service that creates components and template views at the application root.

methods.content:
/**
 * Creates and inserts a component instance into the application's root ViewContainerRef.
 *
 * @typeParam T - Component class type
 * @param content - Component type to instantiate
 * @returns ComponentRef<T> - Reference to the created component
 */
createFromComponent<T>(content: AXComponentType<T>): ComponentRef<T>

/**
 * Creates and inserts an embedded view from a TemplateRef into the application's root ViewContainerRef.
 *
 * @typeParam T - Template context type
 * @param content - TemplateRef to render
 * @returns EmbeddedViewRef<T> - Reference to the created embedded view
 */
createFromTemplate<T>(content: TemplateRef<T>): EmbeddedViewRef<T>

types.title: AXComponentType<T>
types.description: Type constraint for a component class constructor.

props.name: new (...args)
props.description: Constructor signature of the target component class.

types.title: AXComponentContent<T>
types.description: Union type accepted by higher-level services such as AXOverlayService.

props.name: content
props.description: Either a component class or a template reference.

types.title: AXComponentInputs
types.description: Key-value map of input names to values passed when creating dynamic components.

props.name: [key: string]
props.description: Input name mapped to its runtime value.

types.title: AXComponentOptions
types.description: Optional configuration when rendering dynamic content.

props.name: inputs?
props.description: Initial input values to set on the created component via setInput().

sections.title: Overview
sections.description: How AXComponentService works under the hood.

content.data:
AXComponentService uses Angular's ApplicationRef to locate the first bootstrapped application component and obtain its root ViewContainerRef. From there it can instantiate any component class or render any TemplateRef at runtime.

This is useful when you need to inject UI outside the normal component tree — for example global overlays, programmatic dialogs, or portal-like rendering. Internally, AXOverlayService and AXPopupComponent rely on this service to create their content.

sections.title: Basic Setup
sections.description: Inject the service and import from the secondary entry point.

sections.title: Creating a Component
sections.description: Dynamically instantiate a component class and access its instance.

sections.title: Creating from a Template
sections.description: Render an ng-template as an embedded view at the application root.

sections.title: Setting Inputs
sections.description: Pass data to dynamically created components using setInput().

sections.title: Moving Content to a Container
sections.description: Attach the dynamically created host element to a specific DOM container — the pattern used by AXPopupComponent.

sections.title: Cleanup and Lifecycle
sections.description: Destroy dynamic content when it is dismissed to prevent memory leaks.

sections.title: When to Use Higher-level Services
sections.description: Prefer AXOverlayService for positioned overlays, backdrops, and z-index management.
